transformation_type: glycosylation via glycosyl sulfoxide activation to glycosyl triflates followed by stereocontrolled O-, S-, or N-glycoside formation
mechanism_type: activation of glycosyl sulfoxides to glycosyl triflates with triflic anhydride or related activators followed by nucleophilic substitution with anomeric control
product_pattern_text: O-glycoside or N-glycoside with alpha/beta stereocontrol
common_conditions: low temperature activation, often -90 to -24 °C, with hindered base or acid scavenger
synonym_names: Kahne Glycosylation|Kahne glycosidation (sulfoxide method)|reverse Kahne glycosidation
description_short: Sulfoxide-based glycosylation method that activates glycosyl sulfoxides to glycosyl triflates and couples them with hindered O- or N-nucleophiles with high stereocontrol.

transformation_type: enantioselective allylation of aldehydes to homoallylic alcohols using allylstannanes or allylsilanes under chiral Lewis-acid catalysis
mechanism_type: BINOL/Ti(IV) catalyst activation of aldehydes followed by allyl transfer from allyltributylstannane and catalyst turnover through transmetallation
reactant_pattern_text: aldehyde + allyltributylstannane or allylsilane under BINOL/Ti(IV) catalysis
common_conditions: low-temperature Lewis-acid catalysis with 4A molecular sieves and catalytic titanium/BINOL complex
description_short: Catalytic enantioselective allylation platform that converts aldehydes into chiral homoallylic alcohols using allylstannanes and BINOL/Ti(IV) Lewis-acid catalysts.

transformation_type: intramolecular macrolactonization of hydroxy acids to medium- and large-ring lactones using carbodiimide activation with DMAP/DMAP.HCl or EDCI
mechanism_type: carbodiimide-mediated activation of hydroxy acids to acyl intermediates followed by DMAP-assisted cyclization under high dilution
reactant_pattern_text: hydroxy acid or seco-acid precursor under DCC/DMAP/DMAP.HCl or EDCI activation
common_conditions: high dilution, syringe-pump addition, often refluxing chlorinated solvent with carbodiimide and DMAP additives
description_short: Macrolactonization protocol for cyclizing hydroxy acids to macrocycles using carbodiimide activation and DMAP/DMAP.HCl mediation under high-dilution conditions.

transformation_type: radical allyl transfer from allyltributyltin to alkyl halides or related radical precursors
mechanism_type: radical chain process initiated by AIBN, benzoyl peroxide, heat, or photolysis with allyltributyltin as chain-transfer reagent
reactant_pattern_text: alkyl halide, selenide, or related radical precursor + allyltributyltin under AIBN/heat
product_pattern_text: allylated alkane or allyl-substituted carbon framework
common_conditions: radical conditions, typically around 80 °C, with AIBN or peroxide initiator and excess allyltributyltin
description_short: Free-radical allylation method that replaces halides or related radical leaving groups with allyl fragments using allyltributyltin under chain conditions.

transformation_type: amine- or base-catalyzed condensation of aldehydes or ketones with active methylene compounds to electron-poor alkenes
mechanism_type: iminium/enamine or aldol-type activation of carbonyl compounds and methylene donors followed by dehydration to alpha,beta-unsaturated products
reactant_pattern_text: aldehyde or ketone + active methylene compound such as malonate, cyanoacetate, nitrile, or 1,3-dicarbonyl derivative
product_pattern_text: alpha,beta-unsaturated carbonyl or related activated alkene
common_conditions: weak base or amine catalysis, often with azeotropic dehydration or mild heating to promote elimination
description_short: Condensation of carbonyl compounds with active methylene donors under basic or amine catalysis to form alpha,beta-unsaturated carbonyl derivatives and related activated alkenes.
